summary: Matched the Dolphin DVD library units dvd.c and dvdlow.c, moving both from NonMatching to Matching in configure.py. The PR substantially rewrote DVD state-machine/error/cancel handling and low-level DI command scheduling, added timeout/workaround alarm logic, corrected DVD public/internal declarations, and updated symbols for newly identified DVD BSS/SBSS objects. The decomp.dev bot reported +11220 bytes matched code and 42 new function matches, with dvderror.c's __DVDStoreErrorCode slightly improved by correcting ErrorTable size.

key_files.role: Main DVD state-machine matching work; added fatal error/timeouts, cancel helper, reset preparation, DVDCheckDisk, and callback/error-path refinements.
Low-level DI command matching work; added DVDLowCallback, command queue, read workaround logic, timeout alarms, StopAtNextInt/Breaking handling, and __DVDInitWA.
Internal prototypes added for state/error callbacks and new cross-unit functions such as __DVDPrepareResetAsync and __DVDInitWA.
Public API prototype cleanup for DVDCancelAllAsync and DVDCancelAll using DVDCBCallback and explicit void.
Symbol map updated for DVD BSS/SBSS discoveries: AlarmForBreak, Prev, Curr, tmpBuffer size, LastReadFinished, and LastReadIssued.
Changed dolphin/dvd/dvdlow.c and dolphin/dvd/dvd.c from NonMatching to Matching.
Adjusted ErrorTable length from 18 to 16, improving __DVDStoreErrorCode match percentage.

what_changed: Set Object(Matching, "dolphin/dvd/dvdlow.c") and Object(Matching, "dolphin/dvd/dvd.c") in the DolphinLib("dvd") build configuration.
Added DVD initialization details in DVDInit, including OSInitAlarm() and __DVDInitWA(), alongside existing DVD filesystem and interrupt setup.
Reworked dvd.c error handling around stateError(int code), cbForStateError, stateTimeout, cbForUnrecoveredError, and cbForUnrecoveredErrorRetry, including explicit __DVDStoreErrorCode calls.
Introduced FatalErrorFlag and recurring intType == 0x10 timeout/fatal-error handling in many DVD state callbacks.
Factored repeated cancellation completion code in dvd.c into static inline BOOL cancel_inline(int resume).
Adjusted stateCheckID behavior so default read/seek-style commands compare tmpBuffer against currID, while DVD_COMMAND_CHANGE_DISK keeps the disk-change path.
Added dvd.c alarm-based cover-close recovery through static OSAlarm alarm, AlarmHandler, OSCreateAlarm, and OSSetAlarm(OS_MSEC_TO_TICKS(1150), AlarmHandler).
Implemented or matched DVDCheckDisk and __DVDPrepareResetAsync near the end of dvd.c.

Added dvdlow.c DVDCommand queue state, CommandList[3], AlarmForWA, AlarmForTimeout, AlarmForBreak, Prev/Curr read tracking, StopAtNextInt, WorkAroundType, WorkAroundSeekLocation, LastReadFinished, LastReadIssued, LastCommandWasRead, NextCommandNumber, and FirstRead.
Added low-level DVD read workaround flow in dvdlow.c: ProcessNextCommand, Read, HitCache, DoJustRead, SeekTwiceBeforeRead, WaitBeforeRead, and __DVDLowSetWAType.
Added timeout infrastructure in dvdlow.c through AlarmHandlerForTimeout and SetTimeoutAlarm, with most DVDLow* commands now arming a 10-second timeout and large reads using 20 seconds.
Changed DVDLowBreak from direct DI status-register break manipulation to StopAtNextInt = TRUE and Breaking = TRUE.
Updated low-level commands to use sizeof(DVDDiskID) and sizeof(DVDDriveInfo) instead of repeated 0x20 literals.
Changed public prototypes to int DVDCancelAllAsync(DVDCBCallback callback) and long DVDCancelAll(void).
Updated GALE01 symbols to identify AlarmForBreak, Prev, Curr, tmpBuffer as size 0x80, and LastReadFinished/LastReadIssued as 8-byte OSTime-like objects.
smart_moves: The PR did not only tweak expressions for isolated functions; it reconstructed missing SDK control flow across dvd.c and dvdlow.c, enabling entire compilation units to be promoted to Matching.
Factoring repeated cancel-completion behavior into cancel_inline(int resume) reduced duplicated state-machine code while preserving resume-point differences needed for matching.
Adding explicit timeout handling via intType == 0x10, AlarmForTimeout, and stateTimeout aligned high-level DVD state handling with low-level DI timeout delivery.
Using a small DVDCommand queue in dvdlow.c reproduced hardware workaround sequencing such as seek-before-read and delayed read scheduling instead of forcing all reads through one path.
Updating symbols.txt alongside code was necessary because the matching work depended on correct object boundaries and types, especially for OSAlarm objects and 8-byte OSTime values.
Header cleanup used existing typedefs such as DVDCBCallback rather than open-coded callback signatures, improving consistency with Dolphin SDK-style declarations.
decomp_lessons: For Dolphin SDK library work, matching a source file may require reconstructing interlocking state machines across files, not just local codegen fixes.

Timeout paths can be encoded as synthetic interrupt causes; here low-level AlarmHandlerForTimeout calls the saved DVDLow callback with 0x10, and dvd.c state callbacks treat intType == 0x10 as fatal timeout handling.
Object-size corrections in symbols.txt matter for matching: LastReadFinished and LastReadIssued were modeled as 8-byte values, and tmpBuffer's true allocation overlapped prior unknown BSS space.
When a repeated state-machine idiom appears in many callbacks, an inline helper can be the matching shape if it keeps state writes, callback order, and resume values exact.
Prefer SDK typedefs and explicit void parameter lists in headers when they match surrounding conventions; these small signature differences can affect matching and readability.
Use sizeof(named struct) for DI transfer lengths when the SDK type is known; it documents intent and can still compile to the expected constant.
Low-level hardware code often clears or snapshots callbacks before invoking them; dvdlow.c copies Callback to a local, clears the global, then calls it.
Alarm setup and cancellation are part of observable code shape in SDK I/O routines; missing OSCreateAlarm/OSSetAlarm/OSCancelAlarm calls can block matches across many command wrappers.
naming_conventions: High-level DVD state callbacks use cbForState* names, e.g. cbForStateError, cbForStateGettingError, cbForStateGoToRetry, cbForStateCheckID1, and cbForStateBusy.
Unrecovered error helpers follow cbForUnrecoveredError and cbForUnrecoveredErrorRetry.
Low-level DVD API functions use DVDLow* names, e.g. DVDLowRead, DVDLowSeek, DVDLowStopMotor, DVDLowRequestError, DVDLowInquiry, and DVDLowAudioBufferConfig.
Internal Dolphin DVD functions use double-underscore prefixes, e.g. __DVDInitWA, __DVDPrepareResetAsync, __DVDInterruptHandler, and __DVDLowSetWAType.
BSS/SBSS SDK globals in this slice use PascalCase names such as CommandList, AlarmForTimeout, AlarmForBreak, Prev, Curr, LastReadFinished, LastReadIssued, LastCommandWasRead, and NextCommandNumber.
Callback typedefs are preferred where available: DVDCBCallback for public DVD command callbacks and a local DVDLowCallback for low-level unsigned interrupt-cause callbacks.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Promoted whole objects to Matching only after code, declarations, and symbol map were updated together.
Introduced static inline cancel_inline(int resume) to reproduce repeated cancellation codegen without leaving bulky duplicated blocks.

Used volatile qualifiers for hardware/state globals in dvdlow.c, including StopAtNextInt, WaitingCoverClose, Breaking, WorkAroundType, LastReadFinished, LastReadIssued, LastCommandWasRead, and Reset-related state.
Matched DI register writes directly through __DIRegs indices while preserving command constants such as 0xa8000000, 0xAB000000, 0xE0000000, 0xE1000000, 0xE2000000, 0xE3000000, and 0xE4000000.
Modeled read timing with OSTime and conversion helpers such as OSSecondsToTicks, OSMillisecondsToTicks, OSMicrosecondsToTicks, and OS_MSEC_TO_TICKS.
Adjusted data declarations to align with generated storage: OSAlarm objects in BSS, Prev/Curr as small structs, tmpBuffer alignment/size, and 8-byte OSTime SBSS values.
Used boolean constants TRUE/FALSE and BOOL returns in places where the SDK style and codegen expected them.

review_feedback.feedback: GALE01 matched code increased to 29.69% (+0.29%, +11220 bytes) and linked code increased to 21.96% (+0.33%, +12732 bytes).

Reported 42 new matches, including cbForStateBusy, DVDLowRead, cbForStateGettingError, __DVDInterruptHandler, __DVDPrepareResetAsync, Read, cbForStateGoToRetry, DVDCheckDisk, cbForStateCheckID1, cbForStateCheckID3, stateReady, cbForStateError, and multiple DVDLow command wrappers.
Reported one improvement in an unmatched function: main/dolphin/dvd/dvderror __DVDStoreErrorCode improved from 40.66% to 45.35%.
No human review comments were present in the provided slice.

follow_up_queries: Find PRs that matched remaining dolphin/dvd/dvderror.c or changed __DVDStoreErrorCode after ErrorTable size 16.
Search for later edits to DVDLowRead workaround logic, WorkAroundType, or __DVDLowSetWAType.
Compare other Dolphin SDK low-level I/O files for timeout alarm patterns using callback cause 0x10.
Look for PRs that adjusted GALE01 symbols around CommandList, AlarmForTimeout, tmpBuffer, LastReadFinished, and LastReadIssued.
Search future DVD state-machine fixes that reuse cancel_inline-style factoring or intType == 0x10 fatal timeout handling.
